2606.05532.title: Individual Gain, Collective Loss: Metacognitive Adaptation in AI-Assisted Creativity
2606.05532.paper_summary: Recent studies reveal a paradox: AI enhances individual creative outputs while reducing collective diversity. Current explanations -- cognitive offloading and over-reliance -- identify symptoms but not mechanisms. We propose selective metacognitive adaptation: routine AI use redistributes rather than uniformly diminishes metacognitive effort. Some capacities are amplified (partner modeling, surface control), while others are systematically under-supported (originality evaluation, reflective integration). This redistribution explains both individual satisfaction and collective convergence. We present a taxonomy of six metacognitive capacities organized by temporal phase, characterize their tendencies under routine AI use, and show how individually rational adaptation produces emergent social costs. The framework generates specific predictions for researchers and design principles for practitioners seeking to preserve both individual creative satisfaction and collective creative diversity.
2606.05532.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究揭示了一個悖論：人工智慧提升了個人的創造性產出，同時降低了集體的多樣性。當前的解釋——認知外包和過度依賴——識別了症狀但未能揭示機制。我們提出選擇性元認知適應：常規使用人工智慧重新分配，而不是均勻減少元認知努力。一些能力被放大（夥伴建模、表面控制），而其他能力則系統性地受到支持不足（原創性評估、反思整合）。這種重新分配解釋了個人滿意度和集體趨同。我們提出了一個按時間階段組織的六種元認知能力的分類法，描述了它們在常規使用人工智慧下的趨勢，並展示了個別理性適應如何產生新興的社會成本。該框架為研究人員生成了具體預測，並為尋求同時保護個人創造性滿意度和集體創造性多樣性的實踐者提供了設計原則。
2606.05532.author: Anna Mikeda et.al.
